symptoms.symptom_name: Explosive widespread pruritic urticarial wheals after a heat trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Widespread pruritic wheals after a physical trigger is the core cutaneous mast-cell presentation.
Urticaria is the skin limb of anaphylaxis but is non-diagnostic alone — it needs a second organ system to become anaphylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dermatographism (linear wheal on stroking / seam pressure)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inducible (dermatographic) urticaria is a hallmark of cutaneous mast-cell reactivity and points away from an infective exanthem.
Viral exanthems are not inducible/wheal-forming on stroking.
Histamine-mediated cutaneous vasodilation causes flushing; consistent with mast-cell activation confined to skin.

symptoms.symptom_name: No throat tightness / normal swallowing / unchanged voice (airway screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of upper-airway involvement removes the most dangerous anaphylaxis limb and is central to excluding it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dyspnoea / no wheeze / speaks full sentences (lower-airway screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No respiratory limb — one of the required systemic involvements for anaphylaxis is absent.

symptoms.symptom_name: No faintness / no presyncope on standing (circulatory screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No cardiovascular (hypotension) limb; the patient is upright and mobile, arguing strongly against distributive shock.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vomiting / no abdominal cramps (GI screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No persistent severe GI limb after exposure — a further systemic involvement absent.
Failure of a standard OTC antihistamine dose indicates a severe flare warranting H1 up-titration plus an H2 antagonist, not a repeat of the same dose.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known background mast-cell / histamine disorder with prior milder flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recognised prior pattern of cutaneous flares from a known mast-cell disorder makes a recurrent cutaneous flare the leading explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sinus tachycardia HR ~105 bpm with normal blood pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia with a normal BP is explained by anxiety, pruritus and histamine vasodilation — NOT distributive shock. Sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory status from the patient-reportable absence of faintness, not a measured BP/HR.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patent airway on oropharyngeal exam; chest clear to auscultation; normotensive with SpO2 99%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician confirmation of a patent airway, clear chest and stable haemodynamics — sealed because it requires physical examination the AI Doctor cannot perform; the AI must reach the same exclusion from the patient-reportable negative screen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum mast-cell tryptase (if drawn near the acute event)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A transient rise in tryptase supports mast-cell mediator release retrospectively; a hospital/lab investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Skin-confined mast-cell activation (anaphylaxis excluded by negative systemic screen)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Widespread urticaria + dermatographism + flushing WITH negative airway, respiratory, circulatory and GI screens defines a skin-limited mast-cell flare and rules out anaphylaxis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports the mast-cell diagnosis and specialist work-up but is an outpatient/hospital test — not required to manage tonight and not obtainable via telehealth in real time.
There is no anaphylaxis (negative multi-system screen); adrenaline is not indicated for a purely cutaneous flare and instructing an autoinjector here is an error of commission.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department transfer / ambulance for the baseline flare
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A stable cutaneous flare with a negative systemic red-flag screen is managed as outpatient; routine ED/000 transfer at baseline is over-triage. (Escalation applies ONLY if airway/circulatory red flags emerge — see node 13.)
